Cell.Data: Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

A migration template (Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file) consists of different sheets which are visible at the bottom of the migration template. You use the different sheets to specify the data that belongs to different data structures. For example the migration template for the migration object 'Product', contains a sheet for basic data, a sheet for plant data, and so on. Some sheets are mandatory, and some are optional.

A migration template is based on the active view of the relevant migration object. You can find information about the active view in the Microsoft Excel XML file. In the file, navigate to File -> Info. You can find the active view name under Properties -> Tags.

In the Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file, navigate to 'File' -> 'Options' -> 'Advanced'. Under the option 'When calculating this workbook:', ensure that the option 'Set precision as displayed' is selected.

The size limit for each file is 100 MB. You can upload multiple XML files at once by using a zip file. Note that the combined size of all the XML files you want to add to the zip file must not exceed 160MB. The maximum permitted size of the zip file is 100MB.

You can increase the size limit for each uploaded XML file to 160MB by changing the system parameter (icm/HTTP/max_request_size_KB). The limit for the zip file is still 160 MB with the system parameter.
The 'Field List' sheet is one of the first sheets in the migration template. You use this sheet to get an overview of the expected data in one central location.
It contains information about the mandatory and optional sheets, as well as detailed information for each sheet (for example the expected data type and length for the fields in each sheet).
On the 'Field List' sheet, you can view the following information for each field in the migration template:
The name of the sheet, and whether it is mandatory or optional. Only mandatory sheets have the suffix 'Mandatory', for example 'Basic Data (Mandatory)'. All other sheets are optional.

You can quickly get an overview of the mandatory and optional sheets by looking at the color of the sheet names at the bottom of the migration template. The name of the mandatory sheets have the color orange, while the optional sheets have the color blue.
The group name for the fields in a sheet.
The individual fields in each sheet, as well as whether fields are mandatory for a sheet.
Information about the expected format of the individual fields, for example the data type and length.

You can quickly get an overview of the mandatory and optional fields by looking at the background color of the cells in each sheet. The cells ofthe mandatory fields have an orange background color, while cells of optional fields have no background color.

In the field list sheet, certain technical information about the fields is hidden by default. The columns 'SAP Structure' and 'SAP Field' (columns 8 and 9) are hidden by default. The column 'SAP Structure' is the technical name of the structure that the field belongs to. The column 'SAP Field' is the technical name of the field. To unhide these columns, select the columns adjacent to either side of the columns that you want to unhide. Right-click your selection, and choose 'Unhide'.
If you want to extract data from SAP ERP, the technical name of the structure and the technical name of the field often corresponds to the SAP ERP table name and field name.
For each migration template, you need to specify data for the mandatory sheets, and for the optional sheets that are relevant for your project:
Mandatory sheets represent the minimum set of data you must provide for data migration. Fill in all mandatory fields.
Use optional sheets depending on your migration scope and available legacy data.
In each sheet, the cells of the mandatory fields have an orange background color, while cells of optional fields have no background color
Viewing Additional Information for Each Column

In row 8, you can view the field names in SAP S/4HANA, as well as additional information such as the expected format (for example the data type and length). Note that you must expand the row to view this additional information.
Some fields are mandatory, and some are optional. The wildcard character (‘*’) beside the name of a field indicates that the field is mandatory.
Although an optional sheet may contain mandatory columns, if the sheet is not relevant for your project, there is no need to fill the mandatory columns in the sheet with data.

Rows 4, 5, and 6 are hidden by default. Row 4 is the technical name of the structure (corresponds to the sheet name). Row 5 is the technical name of the field (corresponds to row 8 - the field description). Row 6 contains technical information such as the data type and length.
To unhide these rows, select the rows adjacent to either side of the rows that you want to unhide. Right-click your selection, and choose 'Unhide'.
You can view the data type for a field in row 8 (see 'Viewing Additional Information for Each Column' above). Depending on the field, one of the following data types may be required:

Letters, numbers, and special characters are permitted. In the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, you can map the values of certain fields with the data type text (usually those fields with Length: 80) to their correct SAP S/4HANA target values. You can do this value mapping in the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit when you start the transfer (in the step 'Convert Values').

Enter numbers with decimals in the relevant country-specific format, for example 12.34 (United States) or 12,34 (Germany). For fields with decimals, the declared length includes decimals (if required), for example if the information for the column states: Length: 8, Decimals: 3, then a number such as 12345.678 is permitted. Note that decimal places are not mandatory. In this example, you can specify a whole number up to length 8 without decimal places, for example ‘1’. This number would be set to ‘1.000’ internally. For negative numbers, ensure that a minus sign ('-') directly precedes the number, for example '-100'. Note that currencies with more than 3 decimal places are not supported.
Note that the maximum field length supported by Microsoft Excel is 15 digits (including decimals). If you have longer numbers, use the option for transferring data to S/4HANA using staging tables.

Enter the date in your country-specific format, for example 12/31/1998 (United States) or 31.12.1998 (Germany). Note that Microsoft Excel automatically recognizes different date formats and transforms them automatically to the correct XML format.
Enter the time in the format HH:MM:SS, for example 02:52:40

When copying data to a sheet from Microsoft Excel, always right-click the relevant cell and choose the paste option 'Values (V)'. Avoid pasting data that includes formatting and formulas into the migration template, as this will corrupt the structure of the XML migration template.
Using the Find and Replace Function

Do not use the Microsoft Excel function 'Find and Replace'. If you change data by using this function, you may also unintentionally change the field names and corrupt the structure of the XML migration template.
Ensure that you only save the migration template as a Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file. Other file types are not supported by the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.
Do not make any changes to the structure of the migration template, specifically:
Do not delete, rename or change the order of any sheet in the migration template.
Do not change the formatting of any cells.
Do not hide, remove, or change the order of any of the columns in the migration template.
Any changes to the sheets will result in a corrupted XML structure. Such modified migration templates are not supported by the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.
Field List for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Unit for Min. Overlap Time(ISO Format)
Net Price in Purchasing Info Record
BOM Usage of Header Material Number
Control Profile for Management of PRT
Standard Value for Quantity of PRT
Formula for Calculate Total Usage Value
Reference Date for Start of PRT Usage
Offset to Start of PRT Usage
Offset Unit for Start of PRT Usage
Reference Date for End of PRT Usage
Offset to Finish of PRT Usage
Offset Unit for End of PRT Usage
Unit of Measure for Activity(ISO Format)
Unit of M. Standard Value 01(ISO Format)
Unit of M. Standard Value 02(ISO Format)
Unit of M. Standard Value 03(ISO Format)
Unit of M. Standard Value 04(ISO Format)
Unit of M. Standard Value 05(ISO Format)
Unit of M. Standard Value 06(ISO Format)
Net Price in Purchasing Info Record
Mode for Reference to Master Inspection Characteristic
Mode for Reference to Master Inspection
Type of Quantitative Characteristic Control Indicators
Indicator: Ref. to Charc. Attribute Rqd.
Type of Qualitative Characteristic Control Indicators
Result Confirmation of Characteristic Control Indicators

Inspection Scope of Characteristic Control Indicators
Docu. Confirmation of Characteristic Control Indicators
Ind.: 0 Is a Lower Specification Limit
Ind.: 0 Is a Upper Specification Limit
Ind.: 0 Is a Lower Plausibility Limit
Ind.: 0 Is a Upper Plausibility Limit
Number of Value Classes for Insp. Result
Assigned Code Group or Selected Set
Plant of the Assigned Selected Set
Defect Code Group for General Rejection
Defect Code Group for Rej. at Lower Tor.
Defect Code for Rej. at Upper Spec. Lim.
Defect Code Group for Rej. at Upp. Toler
Defect Code for Rej. at Upp. Spec. Limit
Char. (in Plan) Whose Q-Level Be Copied
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.
Control Parameter for Lower Level Component Allocation

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

BOM Usage
The BOM Usage controls the selection of the relevant BOM in the component assignment Note: 1. The BOM Usage and Application are used for component allocation 2. The BOM components will be assigned to the first operation by default when creating production order if you have not manually assigned to any operation in the routing. This is the standard functionality.
Type: Text Length: 80

Application
The BOM application, in connection with the BOM usage, controls the selection of the correct alternative BOMs. Note: 1. The BOM Usage and Application are only mandatory for component allocation 2. The BOM components will be assigned to the first operation by default when creating production order if you have not manually assigned to any operation in the routing. This is the standard functionality.
Type: Text Length: 80

Change Number
Number used to uniquely identify a change master record.
Note: please take care when you are using the change number, this number is only used for initial load of routing creation. It’s not for changes.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing. Please be careful when filling value for this field, and also read object documentation first. Note: If not set, the system date of the data migration will be used.
Type: Date

Usage*
Key which distinguishes the work centers by their category (for example production work center, maintenance work center)
Type: Text Length: 80

Status*
You use the status key to indicate the processing status of a task list. For example, you can indicate whether the task list is still in the creation phase or has already been released.
Type: Text Length: 80

From Lot Size
Lower limit of the lot-size range.
The lot size comprises the quantity of a sort or series that is manufactured continuously without switching or interrupting the production process.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

To Lot Size
Upper limit of the lot-size range.
The lot size comprises the quantity of a sort or series that is manufactured continuously without switching or interrupting the production process.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Measure(ISO Format)*
The unit of measure used for the material to be produced in the task list.
Type: Text Length: 80

Dynamic Modification Level
Type: Text Length: 80

Dynamic Modification Rule
Type: Text Length: 80

Header Long Text
This is the complete text of the long text, organized by logon language. The system will automatically divide the text lines into multiple lines. If you provide both description and long text, the description is copied to the first line of the long text. If you enter only a long text and no description, the initial 40 characters of the first line of the long text are copied to the short text. This process is typically used for migrating long text data from legacy SAP systems to the SAP S/4HANA.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.
Assignment of Task Lists to Materials

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing. Please be careful when filling value for this field, and also read object documentation first. Note: if not set, the system date of the data migration will be used.
Type: Date
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence*
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing. Please be careful when filling value for this field, and also read object documentation first. Note: If not set, the system date of the data migration will be used.
Type: Date

Sequence Category*
Key indicating a standard sequence a parallel sequence or an alternative sequence. Blank-Standard sequence, 1-Parallel sequence, 2-Alternative sequence.
Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 80

Reference Sequence
Key which identifies the sequence of operations to which a sequence refers.
Type: Text Length: 6

Alignment Key for Scheduling
Type: Text Length: 80

Number of the Branch Operation
Type: Text Length: 4

Number of the Return Operation
Type: Text Length: 4

From Lot Size
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

To Lot Size
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Sequence Long Text
This is the complete text of the long text, organized by logon language. The system will automatically divide the text lines into multiple lines. If you provide both description and long text, the description is copied to the first line of the long text. If you enter only a long text and no description, the initial 40 characters of the first line of the long text are copied to the short text. This process is typically used for migrating long text data from legacy SAP systems to the SAP S/4HANA.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing. Please be careful when filling value for this field, and also read object documentation first. Note: 1. If not set, the system date of the data migration will be used. 2. If the validity date of operation starts and if the work center has no valid costing data in this time, then the activity type will be missing.
Type: Date

Work Center Plant
Type: Text Length: 80

Control Key*
Determines which business transactions should be executed for the object that belongs to the task list or order (for example scheduling or costing)
Type: Text Length: 80

Operation Short Text
Describes the operation or sub-operation.
Type: Text Length: 40

Base Quantity*
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Measure for Activity(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Denominator
Enter the number of units of the alternative unit of measure (denominator) that corresponds to the number of units of the base unit of measure (numerator). The alternative unit of measure is kilogram (kg). The base unit of measure is piece (PC). 5 kg correspond to 3 pieces. 5 kg = 3 PC = 1 kg = 3/5 PC In this case, the quotient is therefore 3/5 (the numerator being 3 and the denominator 5).
Type: Number Length: 5

Numerator
Enter the number of units of the alternative unit of measure (denominator) that corresponds to the number of units of the base unit of measure (numerator). The alternative unit of measure is kilogram (kg). The base unit of measure is piece (PC). 5 kg correspond to 3 pieces. 5 kg = 3 PC = 1 kg = 3/5 PC In this case, the quotient is therefore 3/5 (the numerator being 3 and the denominator 5).
Type: Number Length: 5

Standard Value 01
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 01(ISO Format)
Unit of Measurement of Standard Value
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 02
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 02(ISO Format)
Unit of Measurement of Standard Value
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 03
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 03(ISO Format)
Unit of Measurement of Standard Value
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 04
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 04(ISO Format)
Unit of Measurement of Standard Value
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 05
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 05(ISO Format)
Unit of Measurement of Standard Value
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 06
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 06(ISO Format)
Unit of Measurement of Standard Value
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Do Not Apply to Order
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Reduction Strategy per Operation
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Teardown and Wait Occur
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Maximum Wait Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for the Max. Wait Time(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Minimum Wait Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Required Wait Time(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Queue Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Std. Queue Time(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Minimum Queue Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Min. Queue Time(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Move Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Std. Move Time(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Minimum Move Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Min. Move Time(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Required Splitting
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Maximum Number of Splits
Type: Number Length: 3

Minimum Processing Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Min. Process Time (ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Required Overlapping
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Optional Overlapping
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Flow Manufacturing
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Minimum Overlap Time
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Min. Overlap Time(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Minimum Send-Ahead Quantity
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Indicator for Relevancy to Costing*
Controls the extent to which a BOM item, operation, or suboperation in the routing is included in costing.
These options as possible: 1. If you want the "Costing Relevancy" to be determine from the "Control Key" on the target system, enter the value "D". 2. If you do not want the operation/sub operation to be relevant to costing, enter the value "N". 3. If you want the operation/sub operation to be relevant to costing, enter the value "X".
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: External Processing Operation
Indicator: External Processing Operation with Subcontracting. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty
Type: Text Length: 1

Number of Purchasing Info Record
Type: Text Length: 80

Purchasing Group for External Processing
Type: Text Length: 80

Sort Term
Subdivides several purchasing info records belonging to a product group into groups.
Type: Text Length: 10

Material Group
Key that you use to group together several products or services with the same attributes, and to assign them to a particular product group.
Type: Text Length: 80

Account Number of Supplier
Type: Text Length: 80

Planned Delivery Time in Days
Number of calendar days needed to obtain the product or service if it is procured externally.
Type: Number Length: 3

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5

Cost Element
Number of the G/L account • Which is to be debited with the goods issue, if you have entered a consumption account, or • for which an account assignment check is to be carried out.
Type: Text Length: 80

Net Price in Purchasing Info Record
Type: Number Length: 23 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Currency Key(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Inspection Point Completion
Flow Variants for Inspection Point Completion In this list field, you can choose one of the following variants for inspection point completion:
' ' - Quantity, valuation and confirmation (production) 1 - Quantity, valuation (production) 2 - Valuation 3 - Automatic valuation based on insp. point definition
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator for Time-Related
Type: Text Length: 1

Time Factor
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Indicator for No Reference
Type: Text Length: 1

Operation Long Text
This is the complete text of the long text, organized by logon language. The system will automatically divide the text lines into multiple lines. If you provide both description and long text, the description is copied to the first line of the long text. If you enter only a long text and no description, the initial 40 characters of the first line of the long text are copied to the short text. This process is typically used for migrating long text data from legacy SAP systems to the SAP S/4HANA.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

BOM Category*
This key defines a BOM according to the object (such as material or equipment) to which it is linked.
Type: Text Length: 80

Root Material Number*
The topmost material number in the bill of material.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant of Root Material*
Type: Text Length: 80

Alternative BOM of Root Material*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Please note that leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

BOM Header Material Number*
If you want to allocate the BOM items of a root material, enter the root material number in this field, ensuring that it matches the value in the Root Material Number template field. If you want to allocate the BOM items of a phantom assembly for the root material, enter the phantom assembly.
Type: Text Length: 80

Alternative BOM of Header Material*
Identifies one BOM in a BOM group. Please note that leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Item ID*
Identifies a BOM item within a BOM group.
Type: Text Length: 8

BOM Item Number
This number refers to the sequence of BOM components.
Please note that it's mandatory for component allocation.
Type: Text Length: 4

BOM Item Category
Type: Text Length: 1

BOM Component Description
Type: Text Length: 40

Indicator: Backflush
Determines whether the backflush indicator is set in the production order. Here, a 3-level hierarchy logic is applied:
1. In the routing, the backflush indicator is set in the component assignment. 2. In the material master record, the backflush indicator is set to "Always Backflush". 3. In the material master record, the backflush indicator is set to "Work Center Decides", and the indicator is set in the work center. Each material component is assigned to an operation in the production order. If a material component is backflushed, the system posts the withdrawal only upon confirmation of the operation. The withdrawal is then posted automatically. Note: This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Valid From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing. Please be careful when filling value for this field. Note: 1. If not set, the system date of the data migration will be used. 2. if the validity date of component allocation starts earlier than the valid from date of BOM, then the components of BOM cannot be exploded and it will return error..
Type: Date

BOM Usage of Header Material Number
This key defines the area (such as engineering/design or production) where a BOM can be used. Please note that it's mandatory for component allocation.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Item Number for Production Resource/Tool*
Number that specifies the sequence in which the production resources/tools allocated to the operation or activity are sorted.
Type: Number Length: 4

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing. Please be careful when filling value for this field, and also read object documentation first. Note: If not set, the system date of the data migration will be used.
Type: Date

Production Resources/Tools Category
Please refer to the documentation of the migration object to determine the Production Resources/Tools categories that are supported.
Type: Text Length: 80

Material Number
Represents the assembly, the module, or the system that you assemble from the call components transmitted and that you deliver.
Type: Text Length: 80

Production Resources and Tools
Type: Text Length: 80

Document Type
Enter a valid Document type that exists in your system. A valid document is defined via the fields: Document Type, Document number, Document version and Document part.
If the document doesn't exist an error message will be thrown: "Document value Document Type-Document number-Document version-Document part is invalid, please check."
Type: Text Length: 80

Document Number
Enter a valid Document number that exists in your system. A valid document is defined via the fields: Document Type, Document number, Document version and Document part.
If the document doesn't exist an error message will be thrown: "Document value Document Type-Document number-Document version-Document part is invalid, please check."
Type: Text Length: 80

Document Part
Enter a valid Document part that exists in your system. A valid document is defined via the fields: Document Type, Document number, Document version and Document part.
If the document doesn't exists an error message will be thrown: "Document value Document Type-Document number-Document version-Document part is invalid, please check."
Type: Text Length: 3

Document Version
Enter a valid Document version that exists in your system. A valid document is defined via the fields: Document Type, Document number, Document version and Document part.
If the document doesn't exists an error message will be thrown: "Document value Document Type-Document number-Document version-Document part is invalid, please check."
Type: Text Length: 2

Equipment Number
Number by which a piece of equipment can be clearly identified.
An individual, physical object that is maintained as an autonomous unit.
Type: Text Length: 80

Control Profile for Management of PRT
Key with which you can specify how production resources/tools should be handled in the task list or in the order.
Production resources/tools can be processed in the following ways: •scheduling •confirming •costing •printing
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Text Key
Key with which you allocate a text (standard text) to a production resource/tool. This text can contain different specifications, for example, information about using the production resource or tool.
Type: Text Length: 80

First Line of Text
First line of the text which was created as a description for production resource/tool.
Type: Text Length: 40

Standard Value for Quantity of PRT
Number of production resources/tools required at the same time in the operation or the activity. This number has nothing to do with the base quantity.
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit for Quantity of PRT
The unit in which the quantities are entered, for example, standard value, total quantity or remaining quantity.
Type: Text Length: 80

Formula for Calculate Total Quantity
Formula for Calculating the Total Quantity of PRT (production resources/tools)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Usage Value for PRT
Determines the wear and tear on the production resources/tools. Usage value refers to the base quantity in the operation.
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Usage Value Unit of PRT
Unit in which the usage value of production resources/tools is defined.
Type: Text Length: 80

Formula for Calculate Total Usage Value
Formula for Calculating the Total Usage Value of PRT
Type: Text Length: 80

Reference Date for Start of PRT Usage
Key you use to specify a date (for example, setup start date or finish date for completing an operation).
Type: Text Length: 80

Offset to Start of PRT Usage
Offset between the reference date and the start of production resource/tool (PRT) usage. •A negative offset indicates a lead time. In this case, the start of PRT usage lies before the reference date. Enter the minus sign after the value of the offset (for example, 45-). •A positive offset indicates a follow-up time. In this case, the start of PRT usage is after the reference date.
Type: Number Length: 5

Offset Unit for Start of PRT Usage
Unit of time in which the offset to the reference date is expressed.
Type: Text Length: 80

Reference Date for End of PRT Usage
Key you use to specify a date (for example, setup start date or processing finish date of an operation).
Type: Text Length: 80

Offset to Finish of PRT Usage
Offset between the reference date and the finish of the production resource/tool usage. •A negative offset shows a preliminary lead time. In this case, the end of production resource usage is before the reference date. Please enter the minus sign after the value of the offset (for example, 45-). •A positive offset shows follow-up time. In this case, the end of production resource usage is after the reference date.
Type: Number Length: 5

Offset Unit for End of PRT Usage
Unit of time in which the offset to the reference date is expressed.
Type: Text Length: 80

Production Resource/Tool Number
Type: Text Length: 33
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Sub Operation/Activity Number*
Type: Text Length: 4

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing. Please be careful when filling value for this field, and also read object documentation first. Note: 1. If not set, the system date of the data migration will be used. 2. If the validity date of operation starts and if the work center has no valid costing data in this time, then the activity type will be missing.
Type: Date

Work Center Plant
Type: Text Length: 80

Operation Short Text
Type: Text Length: 40

Base Quantity
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Measure for Activity(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 01
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 01(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 02
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 02(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 03
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 03(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 04
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 04(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 05
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 05(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Standard Value 06
Type: Number Length: 9 Decimal: 3

Unit of M. Standard Value 06(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator for Relevancy to Costing*
Controls the extent to which a BOM item, operation, or suboperation in the routing is included in costing.
These options as possible: 1. If you want the "Costing Relevancy" to be determine from the "Control Key" on the target system, enter the value "D". 2. If you do not want the operation/sub operation to be relevant to costing, enter the value "N". 3. If you want the operation/sub operation to be relevant to costing, enter the value "X".
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: External Processing Operation
Indicator: External Processing Operation with Subcontracting. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty
Type: Text Length: 1

Number of Purchasing Info Record
Type: Text Length: 80

Purchasing Group for External Processing
Type: Text Length: 80

Sort Term
Subdivides several purchasing info records belonging to a product group into groups.
Type: Text Length: 10

Material Group
Key that you use to group together several products or services with the same attributes, and to assign them to a particular product group.
Type: Text Length: 80

Account Number of Supplier
Type: Text Length: 80

Planned Delivery Time in Days
Type: Number Length: 3

Net Price in Purchasing Info Record
Type: Number Length: 23 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Currency Key(ISO Format)
Type: Text Length: 80

Sub Operation Long Text
This is the complete text of the long text, organized by logon language. The system will automatically divide the text lines into multiple lines. If you provide both description and long text, the description is copied to the first line of the long text. If you enter only a long text and no description, the initial 40 characters of the first line of the long text are copied to the short text. This process is typically used for migrating long text data from legacy SAP systems to the SAP S/4HANA.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.
Mode for Reference to Master Inspection Characteristic
Type of Quantitative Characteristic Control Indicators
Type of Qualitative Characteristic Control Indicators
Result Confirmation of Characteristic Control Indicators
Inspection Scope of Characteristic Control Indicators
Docu. Confirmation of Characteristic Control Indicators

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Inspection Characteristic Number*
The number that explicitly identifies an inspection characteristic within an operation in an inspection plan. Please fill a numeric value in the template.
Type: Text Length: 4

Valid-From Date
Specifies the start date for the validity period of an object (such as material, BOM, or routing). Please be careful when filling value for this field, and also read object documentation first. Note: if not set, the system date of the data migration will be used.
Type: Date

Mode for Reference to Master Inspection
You can create inspection characteristics with or without reference to a master inspection characteristic.
The mode for reference to the master inspection characteristic is required for inspection characteristic migration. These options as possible: - "L" for reference. In this case, the master inspection characteristic has to be completely maintained, you only have to supply those fields that could not be copied from the master inspection characteristic
- "C" for copy the data from the master inspection characteristic into the inspection characteristic
- "N" for create a lockable and unlockable reference to the master inspection characteristic without a data transfer from the master inspection characteristic. In this case manually fill out all fields in sheet 'Characteristics' of migration template.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Quantitative Characteristic
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, it causes the characteristic to be treated as a quantitative characteristic
Type: Text Length: 80

Master Inspection Characteristics
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant
Plant for the Master Inspection Characteristic
Type: Text Length: 80

Short Text
Text of up to 40 characters that describes the object in more detail. This description is also known as short text.
Type: Text Length: 40

Inspection Method
Name that uniquely identifies an inspection method within a plant.
An inspection method describes how to inspect an inspection characteristic. You can assign an inspection method to a master inspection characteristic or directly to an inspection characteristic in an inspection plan.
Use
You can assign as many inspection methods to an inspection characteristic as you want.
Procedure •Select one of the following procedures: •You enter an inspection method in this field. •You search for an inspection method using the input help.
Dependencies
If you want to search for an inspection method in a different plant, enter the plant in the appropriate field. To use an inspection method from the same plant, do not enter a plant in this field.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant for Inspection Method
Key uniquely identifying a plant.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Ref. to Charc. Attribute Rqd.
Set this indicator('X') only in qualitative characteristic. If you set this indicator, the inspection characteristic is marked as an attributive characteristic, whose allowed values are defined as a selected set from catalog 1 (characteristic attributes).
If you do not set this indicator, the inspection characteristic is marked as an alternative characteristic, that can only take the values "Accepted" or "Rejected".
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Upper Specification Limit
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, you must enter an upper specification limit for the inspection characteristic.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Lower Specification Limit
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, you must enter a lower specification limit for the inspection characteristic.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Check Target Value
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator and either the "Upper specification limit" or "Lower specification limit" indicator, the system checks whether the target value lies within the tolerance.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Sampling Procedure Is Reqd.
If you set this indicator, a sampling procedure must be assigned to the characteristic in the task list.
If you do not assign a sampling procedure to the inspection characteristic and you do not define specifications for the inspection scope on a higher level (in the inspection setup in the Quality Management view of the material master), a 100% inspection takes place.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: SPC Characteristic
If you set this indicator, a control chart is run for this characteristic.
Dependencies
If you set this indicator, you must also set the "Sampling procedure" indicator.
Type: Text Length: 1

Recording Type
Characteristic Recording type in the Results confirmation.
Possible entries: ▪ empty Summarized Recording ▪ '+' Single-Value Recording ▪ '*' Classed Recording ▪ '-' No Results Recording
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Defects Recording
Defects Recording Automatically Called Up
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
If you set this indicator and an inspection characteristic is rejected during results recording, the function for recording defects for a characteristic is automatically called up.
Type: Text Length: 1

Characteristic Category
Characteristic category in the Results confirmation.
Possible entries: ▪ 'X' Required Characteristic ▪ empty Optional Characteristic ▪ '+' Required When Previous Required Characteristic Is Accepted ▪ '-' Required When Previous Required Characteristic Is Rejected
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Recg. the Number of Defects
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, you can enter the number of nonconforming units and the number of defects during results recording If you do not set this indicator, you can enter the number of nonconforming units during results recording.
Type: Text Length: 1

Inspection Scope
Possible entries: • = Fixed Inspection Scope • empty Scope Not Fixed • Smaller Inspection Scope Allowed • Larger Inspection Scope Allowed
Type: Text Length: 80

Documentation Required for Insp. Results
Indicator: documentation of inspection results.
Possible entries: ▪ empty Text Documentation of Test Values Is Not Required ▪ '.' Test Values with Valuation R (Reject) Must Be Documented ▪ '+' All Test Values Must Be Documented with Text
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Long-Term Inspection
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Change Doc. During Results
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Measured Values Is Recorded
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
This indicator can only be set to 'X' in the quantitative characeterstic.
If you set this indicator, measured values for this quantitative characteristic have to be inspected and confirmed.
Measured values can be found in the tab "Master Insp. Char. Basic Data".
Type: Text Length: 3

Decimal Places
Number of Places to the Right of a Decimal Point (Accuracy) Note: the supported maxmium decimal places is 10.
Type: Number Length: 3

Unit of Measure
Unit of Measurement, in Which Quantitative Data Is Stored
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: 0 Is a Target Value
There is an indicator field for target value that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a target value, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Target Value
Value of a quantitative characteristic, from which the actual value of the inspection characteristic should deviate as little as possible.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Ind.: 0 Is a Lower Specification Limit
There is an indicator field for lower specification limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a lower specification limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Lower Specification Limit
Lower limiting value for the actual value of an inspection characteristic.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Ind.: 0 Is a Upper Specification Limit
There is an indicator field for upper specification limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a upper specification limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Upper Specification Limit
Upper limit value for the actual value of an inspection characteristic.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Ind.: 0 Is a Lower Plausibility Limit
There is an indicator field for lower plausibility limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a lower plausibility limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Lower Plausibility Limit
Minimum plausible value of a plausibility limit.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Ind.: 0 Is a Upper Plausibility Limit
There is an indicator field for upper plausibility limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a upper plausibility limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Upper Plausibility Limit
Maximum plausible value of a plausibility limit.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Number of Value Classes for Insp. Result
Number of Value Classes for Inspection Results
Type: Number Length: 3

Indicator: 0 Is a Class Width
There is an indicator field for class width that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a class width, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Class Width
Difference between the upper limit and the lower limit of a class.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Indicator: 0 Is a Class Midpoint
There is an indicator field for class midpoint that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a class midpoint, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Class Midpoint
Arithmetic mean of the class limits (upper and lower limits of a class).
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Indicator: 0 Is a 1st Lower Limit
There is an indicator field for 1st lower limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a 1st lower limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

1st Lower Limit
Lower limiting value of a user-specified limit, for example, a warning limit.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Indicator: 0 Is a 1st Upper Limit
There is an indicator field for 1st upper limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a 1st upper limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

1st Upper Limit
Upper limit value of a user-specified limit, for example, a warning limit.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Indicator: 0 Is a 2nd Lower Limit
There is an indicator field for 2nd lower limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a 2nd lower limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

2nd Lower Limit
Lower limiting value of a user-specified limit, for example, a warning limit.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Indicator: 0 Is a 2nd Upper Limit
There is an indicator field for 2nd upper limit that specifies whether the value is zero. If zero is a 2nd upper limit, enter X, else leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

2nd Upper Limit
Upper limit value of a user-specified limit, for example, a warning limit.
Type: Number Length: 16 Decimal: 16

Assigned Code Group or Selected Set
Type: Text Length: 8

Plant of the Assigned Selected Set
Type: Text Length: 80

Assigned Code Group 2
Type: Text Length: 8

Assigned Code Group 3
Type: Text Length: 8

Assigned Code Group 4
Type: Text Length: 8

Assigned Code Group 5
Type: Text Length: 8

Defect Code Group for General Rejection
Type: Text Length: 80

Defect Code for Rejection: General
Type: Text Length: 80

Defect Code Group for Rej. at Lower Tor.
Type: Text Length: 80

Defect Code for Rej. at Upper Spec. Lim.
Type: Text Length: 80

Defect Code Group for Rej. at Upp. Toler
Type: Text Length: 80

Defect Code for Rej. at Upp. Spec. Limit
Type: Text Length: 80

Dynamic Modification Rule
Type: Text Length: 80

Char. (in Plan) Whose Q-Level Be Copied
Type: Number Length: 4

Sampling Procedure
Sampling Procedure in Inspection Characteristic
Type: Text Length: 80

Sample Unit of Measure
Type: Text Length: 80

Base Sample Quantity
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

SPC Criterion
Serves as a criterion for establishing and limiting quality control charts for statistical process control (SPC).
Type: Text Length: 80

(OBSOLETE) Target Value
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) Lower Specification Limit
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) Upper Specification Limit
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) Lower Plausibility Limit
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) Upper Plausibility Limit
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) Class Width
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) Class Midpoint
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) 1st Lower Limit
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16

(OBSOLETE) 1st Upper Limit
This field is obsolete and must not be used anymore. Please use the new field that is replacing this field. The new field has the same label without the term "(OBSOLETE)".
Type: Text Length: 16
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Name of Global Dependency*
This field is used to link the global dependency to the operation. For global dependency, the assignment of following dependency types are supported 1) Selection condition 2) Procedure
Type: Text Length: 80

Sort Counter
You can use this field to define the processing sequence of procedures, if you allocate multiple procedures to one object.
Values inferred by procedures can overwrite each other. For this reason, the point in time at which a procedure is processed can have a decisive effect on the result.
In this field, enter the number you require to define the processing sequence. The procedure with the lowest counter (for example, 0010) is processed first. The procedure with the highest counter is processed last.
Type: Number Length: 4
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Name of Local Dependency*
The local dependency name is technically mandatory for the tool to uniquely identify a local dependency and connect the records of subordinated sheets. Please note that only numerical characters are allowed for local dependency name and it's not migrated, the system automatically allocates a local dependency number after migration.
Type: Number Length: 10

Dependency Type*
For creating local dependency for an operation, the assignment of following dependency types are supported 5) Selection condition 7) Procedure
Type: Text Length: 80

Dependency Status
This status indicates the processing status of the dependency. 1 Released 2 In Preparation 3 Locked
Type: Text Length: 80

Sort Counter
You can use this field to define the processing sequence of procedures, if you allocate multiple procedures to one object.
Values inferred by procedures can overwrite each other. For this reason, the point in time at which a procedure is processed can have a decisive effect on the result.
In this field, enter the number you require to define the processing sequence. The procedure with the lowest counter (for example, 0010) is processed first. The procedure with the highest counter is processed last.
Type: Number Length: 4
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Name of Local Dependency*
The local dependency name is technically mandatory for the tool to uniquely identify a local dependency and connect the records of subordinated sheets. Please note that only numerical characters are allowed for local dependency name and it's not migrated, the system automatically allocates a local dependency number after migration.
Type: Number Length: 10

Language Key*
Please enter here the language of your description (ISO format).
Type: Text Length: 80

Description
Description of the dependency.
Type: Text Length: 30
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Name of Local Dependency*
The local dependency name is technically mandatory for the tool to uniquely identify a local dependency and connect the records of subordinated sheets. Please note that only numerical characters are allowed for local dependency name and it's not migrated, the system automatically allocates a local dependency number after migration.
Type: Number Length: 10

Language Key*
Please enter here the language of your description (ISO format).
Type: Text Length: 80

Text Line
Allows you to provide detailed information for the maintenance order.
The system will separate the long text into several lines automatically.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
Source Data for Migration Object: Routing
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 02.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Key for Task List Group*
Key identifying a group. You can use groups, for instance, to identify two routings that have different production steps for one material.
Key fields are mandatory and must always be filled with a unique key. 1.If you use internal numbering, you can provide the key of your source system or any other unique key. 2.If you use external numbering, the range is set from 00000000 to 49999999. The key is technically mandatory for the tool to internally connect the records of subordinated sheets with the records of the superordinate sheets.
Note: For internal number range, please be aware to set the control parameter(Routing, internal or external numbering) to internal number range before executing the Prepare step.
Type: Text Length: 80

Group Counter*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field.
Type: Text Length: 2

Sequence
Key which identifies a sequence of operations within a routing. Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field. Note that you can only enter alternative or parallel sequences here. You are not permitted to enter standard sequences since these are generated automatically when creating the task list header.
Type: Text Length: 6

Operation/Activity Number*
Note that the data format, leading zero is required for this field if it's a digital value.
Type: Text Length: 4

Name of Local Dependency*
The local dependency name is technically mandatory for the tool to uniquely identify a local dependency and connect the records of subordinated sheets. Please note that only numerical characters are allowed for local dependency name and it's not migrated, the system automatically allocates a local dependency number after migration.
Type: Number Length: 10

Source Code
Maintain the source code for an object dependency here.
Type: Text Length: unrestricted
